comment_text.sn6.5:1.3: “Here” is the realm of divinity. It is characteristic of Indian religion to depict a struggle for supremacy between sages and the gods. From a Buddhist point of view, all the gods are simply beings who did good things in the past and hence will enjoy an exalted status for a while.
comment_text.sn6.5:2.1: He is heavily featured in the Lakkhaṇasaṁyutta starting at SN 19.1.
comment_text.sn6.5:3.1: A Saṁyutta is devoted to him starting with SN 16.1.
comment_text.sn6.5:4.1: See SN 21.11 and SN 54.7. His verses are at Thag 10.3.
comment_text.sn6.5:5.1: A Saṁyutta is devoted to him starting with SN 52.1.
comment_text.sn6.5:8.6: This is not the view stated by the Divinity in the prose narrative at the start. When these verses were quoted by Moggallāna in a list of his great deeds at MN 50:29.3 and Thag 20.1:59.3, the prose narrative is omitted, avoiding this problem. Moreover, both those passages identify the location as “the Hall of Justice before the assembly”, which does not fit the current discourse. It seems the verses and prose may have different origins.

root_text.sn6.5:1.2: Tena kho pana samayena aññatarassa brahmuno evarūpaṁ pāpakaṁ diṭṭhigataṁ uppannaṁ hoti:
root_text.sn6.5:1.3: “natthi so samaṇo vā brāhmaṇo vā yo idha āgaccheyyā”ti.
root_text.sn6.5:1.4: Atha kho bhagavā tassa brahmuno cetasā cetoparivitakkamaññāya—seyyathāpi nāma balavā puriso …pe… tasmiṁ brahmaloke pāturahosi.
root_text.sn6.5:1.5: Atha kho bhagavā tassa brahmuno upari vehāsaṁ pallaṅkena nisīdi tejodhātuṁ samāpajjitvā.
root_text.sn6.5:2.3: Addasā kho āyasmā mahāmoggallāno bhagavantaṁ dibbena cakkhunā visuddhena atikkantamānusakena tassa brahmuno upari vehāsaṁ pallaṅkena nisinnaṁ tejodhātuṁ samāpannaṁ.
root_text.sn6.5:2.4: Disvāna—seyyathāpi nāma balavā puriso samiñjitaṁ vā bāhaṁ pasāreyya, pasāritaṁ vā bāhaṁ samiñjeyya; evameva—jetavane antarahito tasmiṁ brahmaloke pāturahosi.
root_text.sn6.5:2.5: Atha kho āyasmā mahāmoggallāno puratthimaṁ disaṁ nissāya tassa brahmuno upari vehāsaṁ pallaṅkena nisīdi tejodhātuṁ samāpajjitvā nīcataraṁ bhagavato.
root_text.sn6.5:3.3: Addasā kho āyasmā mahākassapo bhagavantaṁ dibbena cakkhunā …pe…
root_text.sn6.5:3.4: disvāna—seyyathāpi nāma balavā puriso …pe… evameva—jetavane antarahito tasmiṁ brahmaloke pāturahosi.
root_text.sn6.5:3.5: Atha kho āyasmā mahākassapo dakkhiṇaṁ disaṁ nissāya tassa brahmuno upari vehāsaṁ pallaṅkena nisīdi tejodhātuṁ samāpajjitvā nīcataraṁ bhagavato.
root_text.sn6.5:4.3: Addasā kho āyasmā mahākappino bhagavantaṁ dibbena cakkhunā …pe… tejodhātuṁ samāpannaṁ.
root_text.sn6.5:4.6: Atha kho āyasmā mahākappino pacchimaṁ disaṁ nissāya tassa brahmuno upari vehāsaṁ pallaṅkena nisīdi tejodhātuṁ samāpajjitvā nīcataraṁ bhagavato.
root_text.sn6.5:5.3: Addasā kho āyasmā anuruddho …pe… tejodhātuṁ samāpannaṁ.
root_text.sn6.5:5.4: Disvāna—seyyathāpi nāma balavā puriso …pe… tasmiṁ brahmaloke pāturahosi.
root_text.sn6.5:5.5: Atha kho āyasmā anuruddho uttaraṁ disaṁ nissāya tassa brahmuno upari vehāsaṁ pallaṅkena nisīdi tejodhātuṁ samāpajjitvā nīcataraṁ bhagavato.
root_text.sn6.5:6.1: Atha kho āyasmā mahāmoggallāno taṁ brahmānaṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn6.5:9.1: Atha kho bhagavā taṁ brahmānaṁ saṁvejetvā—seyyathāpi nāma balavā puriso samiñjitaṁ vā bāhaṁ pasāreyya, pasāritaṁ vā bāhaṁ samiñjeyya; evameva—tasmiṁ brahmaloke antarahito jetavane pāturahosi.
root_text.sn6.5:9.2: Atha kho so brahmā aññataraṁ brahmapārisajjaṁ āmantesi:
root_text.sn6.5:9.3: “ehi tvaṁ, mārisa, yenāyasmā mahāmoggallāno tenupasaṅkama; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ evaṁ vadehi:
root_text.sn6.5:9.4: ‘atthi nu kho, mārisa moggallāna, aññepi tassa bhagavato sāvakā evaṁmahiddhikā evaṁmahānubhāvā;
root_text.sn6.5:9.5: seyyathāpi bhavaṁ moggallāno kassapo kappino anuruddho’”ti?
root_text.sn6.5:9.6: “Evaṁ, mārisā”ti kho so brahmapārisajjo tassa brahmuno paṭissutvā yenāyasmā mahāmoggallāno tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ mahāmoggallānaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn6.5:9.7: “atthi nu kho, mārisa moggallāna, aññepi tassa bhagavato sāvakā evaṁmahiddhikā evaṁmahānubhāvā;
root_text.sn6.5:9.8: seyyathāpi bhavaṁ moggallāno kassapo kappino anuruddho”ti?
root_text.sn6.5:9.9: Atha kho āyasmā mahāmoggallāno taṁ brahmapārisajjaṁ gāthāya ajjhabhāsi:
root_text.sn6.5:11.1: Atha kho so brahmapārisajjo āyasmato mahāmoggallānassa bhāsitaṁ abhinanditvā anumoditvā yena so brahmā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā taṁ brahmānaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn6.5:13.2: Attamano ca so brahmā tassa brahmapārisajjassa bhāsitaṁ abhinandīti.

translation_text.sn6.5:1.2: Now at that time a certain divinity had the following harmful misconception:
translation_text.sn6.5:1.3: “No ascetic or brahmin can come here!”
translation_text.sn6.5:1.4: Then the Buddha knew what that divinity was thinking. As easily as a strong person would extend or contract their arm, he vanished from Jeta’s Grove and reappeared in that realm of divinity.
translation_text.sn6.5:1.5: Then the Buddha sat cross-legged in the air above that Divinity, having entered upon the fire element.
translation_text.sn6.5:2.2: “Where is the Buddha staying at present?”
translation_text.sn6.5:2.3: With clairvoyance that is purified and superhuman, he saw the Buddha seated cross-legged in the air above that Divinity, having entered upon the fire element.
translation_text.sn6.5:2.4: Then, as easily as a strong person would extend or contract their arm, he vanished from Jeta’s Grove and reappeared in that realm of divinity.
translation_text.sn6.5:2.5: Then Mahāmoggallāna—positioning himself in the east, below the Buddha—sat cross-legged in the air above that Divinity, having entered upon the fire element.
translation_text.sn6.5:5.5: positioned himself in the north, below the Buddha, sitting cross-legged in the air above that Divinity, having entered upon the fire element.
translation_text.sn6.5:6.1: Then Mahāmoggallāna addressed that Divinity in verse:
translation_text.sn6.5:7.1: “Good sir, do you still have the same view
translation_text.sn6.5:7.2: that you had in the past?
translation_text.sn6.5:7.3: Or do you see the radiance
translation_text.sn6.5:8.1: “Good fellow, I don’t have that view
translation_text.sn6.5:8.2: that I had in the past.
translation_text.sn6.5:8.5: So how could I say today
translation_text.sn6.5:8.6: that I am permanent and eternal?”
translation_text.sn6.5:9.1: Having inspired a sense of awe in the Divinity, as easily as a strong person would extend or contract their arm, the Buddha vanished from the realm of divinity and reappeared in Jeta’s Grove.
translation_text.sn6.5:9.2: Then that Divinity addressed a member of his retinue,
translation_text.sn6.5:9.3: “Please, good fellow, go up to Venerable Mahāmoggallāna and say to him:
translation_text.sn6.5:9.4: ‘Moggallāna my good fellow, are there any other disciples of the Buddha who have power and might
translation_text.sn6.5:9.5: comparable to the worthies Moggallāna, Kassapa, Kappina, and Anuruddha?’”
translation_text.sn6.5:9.6: “Yes, good fellow,” replied that retinue member. He went to Moggallāna and asked as instructed.
translation_text.sn6.5:9.9: Then Mahāmoggallāna addressed that member of the retinue of Divinity in verse:
translation_text.sn6.5:10.1: “There are many disciples of the Buddha
translation_text.sn6.5:10.3: and have attained psychic power, <j>expert in encompassing minds,
translation_text.sn6.5:10.4: they’re perfected ones with defilements ended.”
translation_text.sn6.5:11.1: Then that member of the retinue of Divinity, having approved and agreed with what Mahāmoggallāna said, went to that Divinity and said to him,
translation_text.sn6.5:12.1: ‘There are many disciples of the Buddha
translation_text.sn6.5:12.3: and have attained psychic power, <j>expert in encompassing minds,
translation_text.sn6.5:12.4: they’re perfected ones with defilements ended.’”
translation_text.sn6.5:13.1: That’s what that member of the retinue of Divinity said.
translation_text.sn6.5:13.2: Satisfied, that Divinity approved what the member of his retinue said.
